theme_notes: Taglines drawn from the 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage (Messi hat-trick, Mbappé brace, England 4-2 Croatia with a Bellingham winner), the landmark US-Iran deal reopening the Strait of Hormuz backed by a $300B investment framework, Sam Mendes' Beatles biopic cast (Mescal, Keoghan) spotted filming in Barcelona, and the Tony Awards crowning Death of a Salesman with six wins.
settled: Clean as the Strait of Hormuz. For now.
Zero balance. Even Messi couldn't score against you today.
The Beatles biopic cast is messier than your finances. Somehow.
youOwe: Less than a stadium beer at the World Cup. More embarrassing somehow.
Mbappé scored twice in one game. You owe less than fifty bucks. Different leagues.
The Tonys had more drama in one night. Your debt barely makes the playbill.
Not enough to matter. Exactly enough to come up at dinner.
England beat Croatia 4-2. You still haven't settled. Bellingham is disappointed.
The US-Iran deal unlocked billions. You can't unlock your payment app.
The $300B US-Iran deal is locked in. Your $300 tab is still a mystery.
That's a Bellingham jersey plus emotional damage. Pick one.
The Tonys lasted longer than your invoice. Six awards. Zero paid.
Iran and America shook hands on a framework. You can't even open Venmo.
Half a grand. Death of a Salesman got six Tonys. You're the salesman.
Messi scored a hat-trick for less drama than you're causing.
The Strait of Hormuz reopened in a day. This debt? Structural.
Barry Keoghan is playing Ringo Starr and you owe four figures. Both are chaos.
Paul Mescal is playing John Lennon for less than this. And he has to do the accent.
The US-Iran deal needed a $300B fund. You need a therapist and a wire transfer.
You're not a partner. You're a sanctions regime with terrible PR.
At these numbers the UN should be involved. And they'd side with your partner.
theyOwe: Owed less than a group-stage ticket stub. File under 'moral victories.'
Small enough to forgive. Smart enough to screenshot.
Less drama than a single Bellingham assist. Still on the books.
Enough for a Bellingham shirt. Demand it. Framed.
The Strait of Hormuz is open for business. Their Venmo is not.
More than a round at a World Cup bar. Less than you've had to hear about it.
Hundreds of dollars. Iran signed a $300B deal. They can't sign a receipt.
They owe you a flight to the World Cup group stage. Or just the cash.
The Tonys gave 'Death of a Salesman' six awards. You gave them six months of credit.
Almost a grand. Iran opened its oil fields faster than they opened their banking app.
England thrashed Croatia 4-2. You've been equally dominant at getting stiffed.
Death of a Salesman found its ending. Your invoice is still in act one.
Four figures. You're not their partner — you're their Strait of Hormuz.
Even Barry Keoghan gets paid on time. And he plays drums on screen.
Paul Mescal took the Lennon role for less. At least he gets billing.
Messi scored a hat-trick in your honor. The universe acknowledges your sacrifice.
For this amount the US and Iran needed a neutral party. You deserve one too.
They don't owe you money. They owe you a summit, sanctions, and a structured exit.
